content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
75	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
76	
77	### Prover Agent
78	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
79	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
80	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
81	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
82	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
83	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
84	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
85	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
86	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
87	
88	### Refactor Agent
89	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
90	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
91	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
92	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
93	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
94	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
95	
96	### Review Agent
97	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
98	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
99	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
100	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
101	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
102	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1	# Refactor Agent
2	
3	You are the refactor agent. You modify definitions, signatures, types, and imports across all `.lean` files under plan agent direction. You can also create/delete/divide files under the plan agent's guidance.
4	
5	## Your Job
6	
7	The plan agent has identified a structural problem and written a directive describing what to change. You execute that directive. The directive contains:
8	- **Problem**: what's wrong
9	- **Mathematical justification**: why the change is correct
10	- **Changes requested**: exact replacements for each definition/signature
11	- **Affected files**: where cascading breakage will occur
12	- **Expected outcome**: what the sorry landscape should look like after
13	
14	Read the mathematical justification carefully — it tells you the intent behind each change, which you need when fixing cascading type mismatches in downstream files.
15	
16	## Rules
17	
18	### Protected declarations
19	
20	Read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. The declarations listed there are the mathematician's read-only surface, **no agent may modify their signature**. As the refactor agent, under the directive you may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. You cannot do any other modification to `archon-protected.yaml`. 
21	
22	### Blueprint-based informal content
23	
24	This project uses a blueprint (plasTeX + `leanblueprint`). Informal proof sketches live in `blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`, one file per Lean source file. The slug mapping is:
25	
26	```
27	Lean file  Algebra/WLocal.lean  →  chapter  blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex
28	Lean file  Core.lean            →  chapter  blueprint/src/chapters/Core.tex
29	```
30	
31	### What you CAN do
32	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports, module structure 
33	- Create new `.lean` files or delete existing ones 
34	- Delete false or wrong declarations
35	- Change quantifier ordering in lemma statements
36	- Add new definitions, structures, or type classes
37	- Insert `sorry` at proof sites broken by your changes
38	
39	### What you MUST do
40	- **Keep all files compiling.** After every change, check compilation with `lean_diagnostic_messages`. If a change breaks downstream proofs, insert `sorry` at the broken sites. The prover will fill them later.
41	- **Follow the plan agent's directive exactly.** Do not improvise beyond what was requested. If you think additional changes are needed, document them in the "Notes for Plan Agent" section of your report but do not make them.
42	- **Document every change** in `task_results/refactor.md` (see Logging below).
43	- **Verify the full project compiles** before finishing. Use `lean_diagnostic_messages` on every file you touched plus files that import from them.
44	- **Ensure that the Lean files reflect the blueprint structure.** The plan agent gave you the directive and updated the blueprint with the intended structure. Your job is to make the necessary changes to the Lean files to match that structure. 
45	
46	### What you MUST NOT do
47	- **Do NOT fill proofs.** If a proof breaks because you changed a definition, insert `sorry` and move on. Proof filling is the prover's job.
48	- **Do NOT edit PROGRESS.md, task_pending.md, task_done.md, or USER_HINTS.md.**
49	- **Do NOT make changes unrelated to the directive.**
50	- **Do NOT modify the names or signatures of protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.** You may move them to a different file, but not rename or re-sign them.
51	- **Do NOT modify the blueprint chapters.** The plan agent updates the blueprint with the intended informal structure and markers; your job is only to make the Lean files match that structure.
52	
53	## Workflow
54	
55	1. Read the plan agent's directive (provided in your prompt)
56	2. Read the **Mathematical justification** section — understand why each change is correct
57	3. Read the blueprint chapters corresponding to the affected files to understand the intended structure and how the changes fit into it
58	4. Read the affected `.lean` files to understand the current state
59	5. Plan your changes: list which files need modification and in what order (modify definitions first, then fix downstream consumers)
60	6. Execute changes file by file, checking compilation after each file
61	7. Handle cascading breakage: when changing a definition in file A breaks file B, fix the type signatures in B and insert `sorry` at broken proofs
62	8. Verify compilation across all affected files
63	9. Write your report to `task_results/refactor.md`
64	
65	## Handling Cascading Changes
66	
67	When you change a definition, expect downstream breakage. Handle it systematically:
68	
69	1. **Type mismatches:** Update signatures to match the new definition. Use the mathematical justification to determine the correct new types. This is your job.
70	2. **Broken proofs:** Insert `sorry`. This is the prover's job.
71	3. **Missing fields (if you changed a structure):** Add the new fields with `sorry` default values, or update construction sites.
72	4. **Import changes:** If you move or rename declarations, update imports in all affected files.
73	
74	## Logging
75	
76	Write your report to `task_results/refactor.md`. This report is the primary communication channel back to the plan agent — be precise and thorough.
77	
78	```markdown
79	# Refactor Report
80	
81	## Status
82	<COMPLETE or INCOMPLETE>
83	<If INCOMPLETE, explain exactly which changes could not be made and why.>
84	
85	## Directive
86	<Copy the Problem and Changes sections from the directive you received.>
87	
88	## Changes Made
89	
90	### File: <path>
91	- **What:** <description of change>
92	- **Why:** <from directive>
93	- **Cascading:** <list of files that broke and were fixed>
94	
95	### File: <path>
96	...
97	
98	## New Sorries Introduced
99	- `<file>:<line>` — <brief description of what proof broke and why>
100	- ...
101	
102	## Compilation Status
103	- <file>: compiles / errors (describe)
104	- ...
105	
106	## Notes for Plan Agent
107	<Anything the plan agent should know:
108	- Unexpected complications encountered
109	- Additional changes you think are needed but did NOT make (per the rules)
110	- Whether the mathematical justification was sufficient to guide cascading fixes
111	- Suggested follow-up refactors for the next iteration>
112	```
113	
114	The **Status** field is critical: if you write `INCOMPLETE`, the plan agent knows it may need to write another directive in the next iteration. If you write `COMPLETE`, the plan agent will proceed to assign provers to the new sorries.
115	
116	## Write Permissions
117	
118	| File | Permission |
119	|------|-----------|
120	| Any `.lean` file | **read + write** |
121	| `task_results/refactor.md` | **write** |
122	| All other state files | **read only** |

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.Functor
7	
8	/-!
9	# The relative Picard functor as an abelian-group-valued presheaf
10	
11	Phase C step 3 setup (per `STRATEGY.md`): the `AddCommGrpCat`-valued variant
12	of the relative Picard functor of Chapter `Picard_Functor.tex`. The
13	underlying-set functor agrees on the nose with the iter-004 `PicardFunctor`;
14	only the codomain changes from `Type u` to `AddCommGrpCat.{u}`. This is the
15	input shape required by `CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf` for the (deferred)
16	étale sheafification of the Picard presheaf.
17	
18	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FunctorAb.tex`.
19	
20	## Status (iteration 004 — prover round)
21	
22	`PicardFunctorAb` is **filled**: the underlying-set quotient
23	`Pic(C ×_k S) / p_S^* Pic(S)` is wrapped through `Additive` (which converts
24	the multiplicative `CommGroup` instance on `Pic` to the `AddCommGroup`
25	instance demanded by `AddCommGrpCat.of`), and the action on morphisms is
26	`PicardFunctor.quotMap` post-composed with `MonoidHom.toAdditive` and bundled
27	via `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`. Functoriality reduces on the nose to
28	`PicardFunctor.quotMap_id` / `PicardFunctor.quotMap_comp` and the
29	multiplicative-to-additive equiv `MonoidHom.toAdditive`.
30	-/
31	
32	set_option autoImplicit false
33	
34	universe u
35	
36	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
37	
38	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
39	
40	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
41	
42	/-- Phase C step 3 codomain change: the `AddCommGrpCat`-valued variant of the
43	relative Picard functor. The underlying-set functor agrees on the nose with
44	`PicardFunctor` (Chapter `Picard_Functor.tex`); only the codomain changes.
45	Wraps `PicardFunctor.quotMap` via `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`, transporting the
46	multiplicative `Pic` quotient through `Additive` to obtain the
47	`AddCommGroup` instance required by `AddCommGrpCat.of`. -/
48	noncomputable def PicardFunctorAb
49	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
50	    (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} where
51	  obj S := AddCommGrpCat.of (Additive
52	    (Pic (Limits.pullback C.hom S.unop.hom) ⧸
53	      (Pic.pullback (Limits.pullback.snd C.hom S.unop.hom)).range))
54	  map {_ _} f :=
55	    AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (MonoidHom.toAdditive (PicardFunctor.quotMap C f.unop))
56	  map_id _ := by
57	    simp only [unop_id, PicardFunctor.quotMap_id, MonoidHom.toAdditive_id,
58	      AddCommGrpCat.ofHom_id]
59	  map_comp f g := by
60	    simp only [unop_comp, PicardFunctor.quotMap_comp]
61	    rw [show MonoidHom.toAdditive
62	        ((PicardFunctor.quotMap C g.unop).comp (PicardFunctor.quotMap C f.unop)) =
63	        (MonoidHom.toAdditive (PicardFunctor.quotMap C g.unop)).comp
64	          (MonoidHom.toAdditive (PicardFunctor.quotMap C f.unop)) from rfl,
65	      AddCommGrpCat.ofHom_comp]
66	
67	/-- Phase C step 3 forget-and-recover: the natural iso recovering
68	`PicardFunctor C` from `PicardFunctorAb C ⋙ forget AddCommGrpCat`. The
69	underlying-set functor of `PicardFunctorAb` agrees on the nose with
70	`PicardFunctor` (via the type-equal `Additive` wrapper of the iter-004
71	closure), so the iso is the identity natural transformation. See blueprint
72	chapter `Picard_FunctorAb.tex` (`def:PicardFunctorAb_forgetCompare`). -/
73	noncomputable def PicardFunctorAb.forgetCompare
74	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
75	    PicardFunctorAb C ⋙ CategoryTheory.forget AddCommGrpCat.{u} ≅ PicardFunctor C :=
76	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by intros; rfl)
77	
78	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `PicardFunctorAb` along the forgetful
79	functor. The `Additive` wrapper inside the iter-004 `PicardFunctorAb` is
80	type-equal to its underlying multiplicative quotient, so applying
81	`forget AddCommGrpCat` recovers `(PicardFunctor C).obj S` on the nose. Tagged
82	`@[simp]` for downstream rewriting. Phase C step 4 polish (iter-007). -/
83	@[simp] lemma PicardFunctorAb_forget_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
84	    (S : (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))ᵒᵖ) :
85	    (CategoryTheory.forget AddCommGrpCat).obj ((PicardFunctorAb C).obj S)
86	      = (PicardFunctor C).obj S :=
87	  rfl
88	
89	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
90

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	prover  (iteration 008 — Track 2 / étale sheafification iteration; refactor will scaffold one new declaration in `Picard/FunctorAb.lean`, then the prover round closes it with a probe-confirmed one-line body)
5	
6	## Stages
7	- [x] init
8	- [x] autoformalize
9	- [ ] prover
10	- [ ] polish
11	
12	## Big-picture context
13	
14	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
15	
16	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). The 9 protected sorries continue to be blocked behind missing Mathlib infrastructure plus, for the three `def`-flavoured declarations (`genus`, `Jacobian`, `ofCurve`), a user-facing decision about whether agents may add the `noncomputable` modifier to a protected declaration (see `task_pending.md`'s "User-facing decision needed" section). Iter-007 closed the consumer-facing polish track on top of iter-006 Phase A step 5; iter-008 is a focused **Track 2 / étale-sheafification** iteration, landing `Scheme.PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified` in `Picard/FunctorAb.lean`.
17	
18	## Iteration 008 — pre-refactor pass (this run)
19	
20	The iter-007 prover round (per `task_results/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.lean.md` and `task_results/Picard_FunctorAb.lean.md`, both archived into `task_done.md`) closed all three Phase A step 5 polish + Phase C step 4 polish sorries honestly. Plan-agent verification (this iter-008 pass) confirms:
21	
22	- **Sorry count**: 13 → 10 (back to baseline: 9 protected + 1 deferred `representable`); verified via `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/`.
23	- **Compilation**: `lean_diagnostic_messages` on `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` and `Picard/FunctorAb.lean` both return `{success: true, items: []}` — zero errors, zero warnings.
24	- **Axioms**: `lean_verify` on `toModuleKPresheaf_obj`, `toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare`, `PicardFunctorAb_forget_obj` returns only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — only standard kernel axioms. No new `axiom` declarations.
25	- **Probe-confirmed bodies adopted verbatim**: `rfl` (`toModuleKPresheaf_obj`), `Iso.refl _` (`toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare`), `rfl` (`PicardFunctorAb_forget_obj`). No deviations from the recorded forms.
26	- **Blueprint**: `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex` and `Picard_FunctorAb.tex` carry the iter-007 statement and proof blocks — review agent will add `\leanok` markers in the iter-008 review session.
27	- **Protected**: `archon-protected.yaml` unchanged.
28	
29	The iter-007 prover round is therefore accepted; the three closures are migrated to `task_done.md`, the iter-007 task_results files are cleared.
30	
31	## Iter-008 plan-agent re-probe summary
32	
33	| Probe | Result | Source |
34	|---|---|---|
35	| `MonoidalCategory X.Modules` | absent (unchanged from iter-002) | `inferInstance` fails — reconfirmed implicitly via the `representable` deferral |
36	| `HasWeakSheafify ((Scheme.etaleTopology.{u}).over (Spec k)) AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` | **PRESENT** (re-confirmed) | `inferInstance` succeeds |
37	| `HasSheafify ((Scheme.etaleTopology.{u}).over (Spec k)) AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` | **PRESENT** (re-confirmed) | `inferInstance` succeeds |
38	| `Scheme.PicardFunctorAb C ⋙ AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{u+1, u}` (universe-lifted presheaf) | typechecks | `noncomputable example` succeeds |
39	| `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.PicardFunctorAb C ⋙ AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{u+1, u})` | typechecks at `Sheaf _ AddCommGrpCat.{max u (u+1)}` | `noncomputable example` succeeds |
40	| `Sheaf.H` parameterisation | **`AddCommGrpCat`-only** (new finding) | `#check @Sheaf.H` shows `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat → ℕ → Type _` |
41	| `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (toModuleKSheaf C) (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` (parallel `H` route) | typechecks; carries `Module k` via `Ext.instModule` | `example` succeeds |
42	| `Module.finrank k _` requires `noncomputable def` | yes — `noncomputable` error if signature is bare `def` | `example` fails with the expected suggestion |
43	
44	**Strategic update — Track 2 ready:** the étale-sheafification probe is one-liner ready. Iter-008 refactor scaffolds `Scheme.PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified`; iter-008 prover round closes it.
45	
46	**Strategic non-update — Track 1 still gated:** Phase A step 6 / Serre finiteness. Two new gating issues surfaced:
47	- (i) `Sheaf.H` is `AddCommGrpCat`-only: closing `genus` requires a parallel `Sheaf.HModule k F n := Ext (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat k)) F n` (~5 LOC). Probe-confirmed but deferred.
48	- (ii) `genus`/`Jacobian`/`ofCurve` require `noncomputable def` modifier; the original `challenge.lean` writes `def`. **User-facing decision** — flagged in `task_pending.md`.
49	
50	## Current Objectives (iter-008 prover round)
51	
52	Files to attack (one objective per file):
53	
54	1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean`** — close 1 new sorry scaffolded by the iter-008 refactor:
55	
56	   - **L?? `Scheme.PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified`** (`noncomputable def`, signature `(C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) : Sheaf ((Scheme.etaleTopology.{u}).over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) AddCommGrpCat.{max u (u+1)}`). Probe-confirmed body:
57	
58	     ```lean
59	     (CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
60	       (PicardFunctorAb C ⋙ AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{u+1, u})
61	     ```
62	
63	     The naive `AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor` does *not* unify under the `u+1 =?= max u v` constraint; the explicit universe annotation `.{u+1, u}` is required. The probe (`lean_run_code` against current Mathlib) confirms the body typechecks end-to-end.
64	
65	   Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FunctorAb.tex` (`def:PicardFunctorAb_etaleSheafified` block in the new "Étale sheafification" section). 9	# The relative Picard functor
10	
11	Phase C step 2 (per `STRATEGY.md`): the relative Picard functor of a curve
12	`C` over `Spec k`, packaged as a contravariant functor on schemes-over-`k`,
13	plus the (deferred) representability theorem whose closure jointly unblocks
14	the four `Jacobian.lean` sorries.
15

18	## Status (iteration 005 — first prover round)
19	
20	`PicardFunctor` is **filled**: for `C : Over (Spec k)` the functor sends each
21	`S : Over (Spec k)` to the cokernel-as-quotient
22	`Pic(C ×_k S) / p_S^* Pic(S)` of the pull-back homomorphism along the
23	projection `p_S : C ×_k S → S`. Functoriality in `S` is via the universal map
24	of fiber products and `Pic.pullback`.
25	
26	`PicardFunctor.representable` is intentionally left as `sorry` — see the
27	forward-compatibility note below.
28	
29	## Forward-compatibility note (`LineBundle` approximation)
30

input.old_string:
## Status (iteration 004 — prover round)

`PicardFunctorAb` is **filled**: the underlying-set quotient
`Pic(C ×_k S) / p_S^* Pic(S)` is wrapped through `Additive` (which converts
the multiplicative `CommGroup` instance on `Pic` to the `AddCommGroup`
instance demanded by `AddCommGrpCat.of`), and the action on morphisms is
`PicardFunctor.quotMap` post-composed with `MonoidHom.toAdditive` and bundled
via `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`. Functoriality reduces on the nose to
`PicardFunctor.quotMap_id` / `PicardFunctor.quotMap_comp` and the
multiplicative-to-additive equiv `MonoidHom.toAdditive`.
-/
input.new_string:
## Status (iteration 004 — prover round)

`PicardFunctorAb` is **filled**: the underlying-set quotient
`Pic(C ×_k S) / p_S^* Pic(S)` is wrapped through `Additive` (which converts
the multiplicative `CommGroup` instance on `Pic` to the `AddCommGroup`
instance demanded by `AddCommGrpCat.of`), and the action on morphisms is
`PicardFunctor.quotMap` post-composed with `MonoidHom.toAdditive` and bundled
via `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom`. Functoriality reduces on the nose to
`PicardFunctor.quotMap_id` / `PicardFunctor.quotMap_comp` and the
multiplicative-to-additive equiv `MonoidHom.toAdditive`.

## Status (iteration 008 — étale-sheafification scaffold)

`PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified` is **scaffolded**: the étale-topology
sheafification of `PicardFunctorAb`, packaged as a sheaf on
`Scheme.etaleTopology.{u}` over `Spec k` valued in `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}`.
The body post-composes with `AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{u+1, u}` to bridge
the universe gap between the iter-004 codomain `AddCommGrpCat.{u}` and the
value-universe `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` at which `HasSheafify` is inferable in
current Mathlib. The closure body is a one-liner against
`CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf`; the prover round will install it.
-/

input.content:
# Refactor Report

## Status
COMPLETE

## Directive

### Problem
Iter-008 lands the étale-sheafified `PicardFunctorAb` on top of the iter-004
/ iter-005 / iter-007 codomain-change-and-polish chain in
`Picard/FunctorAb.lean`. The previously-blocking Mathlib gap
(`HasSheafify` for `Scheme.etaleTopology.{u}` valued in `AddCommGrpCat`)
has CLOSED in current Mathlib at value-universe `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}`,
modulo a universe-lift of the iter-004 `PicardFunctorAb` (lands at
`AddCommGrpCat.{u}`) via `AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{u+1, u}`. The
directive scaffolds one new `noncomputable def` —
`Scheme.PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified` — with body `:= sorry`. The
prover round will install the probe-confirmed one-liner.

### Changes requested
1. Append `Scheme.PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified` to
   `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean` after the iter-007
   `PicardFunctorAb_forget_obj` simp lemma, body `:= sorry`, signature
   per directive.
2. Refresh the file docstring with an iter-008 status paragraph.
3. Universe-pinning sanity-check via a `lean_run_code` probe.
4. Touch nothing else (in particular, no other file).
5. Sorry budget: `10 → 11` (one new scaffold).
6. Compilation invariant: only one new `declaration uses sorry`
   warning, zero errors.
7. `archon-protected.yaml` unchanged (no protected declaration is
   touched).

## Changes Made

### File: AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean

- **What:** (a) Extended the file docstring with an "iteration 008 —
  étale-sheafification scaffold" status paragraph describing the new
  declaration. (b) Appended a new `noncomputable def
  PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified` after the iter-007
  `PicardFunctorAb_forget_obj` lemma (now at L99–L113), inside the
  existing `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`, body `:= sorry`. The
  signature is verbatim from the directive:
  ```lean
  noncomputable def PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified
      (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
      Sheaf ((Scheme.etaleTopology.{u}).over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
        AddCommGrpCat.{max u (u+1)} :=
    sorry
  ```
- **Why:** Phase C step 3 *proper* — the étale-sheafification of
  `PicardFunctorAb` is the input shape required downstream by
  Grothendieck representability; the new Mathlib `HasSheafify` instance
  unblocks the one-liner. The post-composition with
  `AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{u+1, u}` (which the prover's body will
  use) bridges the universe gap between `AddCommGrpCat.{u}`
  (iter-004 codomain) and `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` (where `HasSheafify`
  inferes); naive `AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor` does not unify under
  `u+1 =?= max u v`, which is why explicit `.{u+1, u}` is required.
- **Cascading:** none. Only `AlgebraicJacobian.lean` (the aggregator
  with `import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.FunctorAb`) and the file itself
  reference it; the new declaration is purely additive.

The iter-004 `PicardFunctorAb` (now at L48), the iter-005
`PicardFunctorAb.forgetCompare` (now at L73), and the iter-007
`PicardFunctorAb_forget_obj` (now at L94) are untouched — verified
both by inspection and by the same line-content as before the edit
(only the docstring grew, shifting line numbers by +14 lines from
their pre-edit positions of L48 / L73 / L83).

No other file was modified.

## New Sorries Introduced
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean:113` —
  `PicardFunctorAb.etaleSheafified` body. Probe-confirmed closure:
  `(CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
   (PicardFunctorAb C ⋙ AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{u+1, u})`.

Net sorry count change: `10 → 11` (matches directive §5).

## Compilation Status

- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FunctorAb.lean`:
  `lean_diagnostic_messages` returns one item, severity=warning,
  `"declaration uses 'sorry'"` at line 109 col 19 (the new
  declaration). **Zero errors. Zero unrelated warnings.** Matches
  directive §6 invariant exactly.
- Probe (directive §3): `lean_run_code` against current Mathlib with
  the prover's intended one-liner closure body returned
  `{success: true, diagnostics: []}` — confirms the signature is
  well-formed and the body that the prover will install typechecks
  end-to-end at the current Mathlib snapshot.
- The aggregator `AlgebraicJacobian.lean` returned a transient
  `success: false` from `lean_diagnostic_messages` (no items,
  intermittently with `failed_dependencies: ["AbelJacobi.lean"]`),
  which appears to be an LSP load/cache hiccup unrelated to the
  refactor — `Picard/FunctorAb.lean` itself diagnoses cleanly, and
  the only file modified is `Picard/FunctorAb.lean`. No file other
  than the one I touched is reachable as a downstream consumer of
  the new declaration in the existing import graph.
- `archon-protected.yaml`: unchanged.

## Notes for Plan Agent

- **Mathematical justification was sufficient.** The directive
  spelled out the universe pinning gymnastic (`u+1 =?= max u v`
  unification trap, the explicit `.{u+1, u}` annotation), the
  signature line by line (including `AddCommGrpCat.{max u (u+1)}` —
  Lean reduces this to `AddCommGrpCat.{u+1}` automatically), and
  flagged that no `set_option linter.style.longLine false` is
  needed inside the declaration. All of these matched what I saw in
  the file.
- **Probe-confirmed body for the prover round** (recorded in
  `PROGRESS.md` and re-confirmed by my §3 probe):
  ```lean
  (CategoryTheory.presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
    (PicardFunctorAb C ⋙ AddCommGrpCat.uliftFunctor.{u+1, u})
  ```
  Inside the namespace, `Scheme.PicardFunctorAb` resolves as
  `PicardFunctorAb` directly. The probe ran from a top-level
  `open ... AlgebraicGeometry` context (not the namespace), where
  the qualified name is `Scheme.PicardFunctorAb` — the prover should
  drop the `Scheme.` prefix when installing the body inside
  `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`.
- **No additional refactors needed.** The directive's "Do NOT touch"
  surface (iter-004 `PicardFunctorAb`, iter-005
  `PicardFunctorAb.forgetCompare`, iter-007
  `PicardFunctorAb_forget_obj`) is preserved verbatim. The file
  docstring's pre-existing iter-004 status paragraph is untouched;
  the iter-008 paragraph is appended after it.
- **Blueprint not modified** (per refactor-agent rules; review agent
  owns markers).
- **No follow-up refactor needed for iter-008.** The file is in a
  clean shape for the prover round.
